patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v3 2/4] locking/percpu-rwsem: Extract __percpu_up_read()
patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch extracts the slowpath of percpu_up_read() into a new function __percpu_up_read(). The original percpu_up_read() is simplified to call this new function when necessary, reducing binary size by avoiding inlining. A tracepoint can now be added to the __percpu_up_read() function without increasing the binary size. This change improves maintainability and flexibility for future development.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v3 3/4] locking: Add contended_release tracepoint to sleepable locks

version_history.subject: [PATCH v3 3/4] locking: Add contended_release tracepoint to sleepable locks

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch adds a new tracepoint called contended_release to the Linux kernel's locking subsystem. The contended_release tracepoint fires on the holder side when a contended lock is released, allowing for correlation of lock hold time under contention with waiter events by lock address. The patch modifies various slowpath unlock paths in sleepable locks (mutex, rtmutex, semaphore, rwsem, percpu-rwsem, and RT-specific rwbase locks) to fire the tracepoint before or after the lock is released, depending on the specific lock type. This enables more detailed tracing of locking behavior.

patches_submitted.subject: [RFC PATCH v3 4/4] locking: Add contended_release tracepoint to spinning locks

version_history.subject: [RFC PATCH v3 4/4] locking: Add contended_release tracepoint to spinning locks

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch adds a contended_release tracepoint to spinning locks, specifically queued spinlocks and rwlocks. The tracepoint is designed to minimize the impact on the hot path when enabled, using an out-of-line function that combines contention check, trace call, and unlock operations. When disabled, only a single NOP instruction is added to the hot path. The patch also includes changes to handle inlined unlock cases, which could not be achieved through Kconfig options alone.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v3 1/4] tracing/lock: Remove unnecessary linux/sched.h include

version_history.subject: [PATCH v3 1/4] tracing/lock: Remove unnecessary linux/sched.h include

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch removes an unnecessary include statement from the lock tracing header file, as none of the trace events in this file reference anything from linux/sched.h.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v3 0/4] locking: contended_release tracepoint instrumentation
patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new tracepoint called contended_release, which fires on the lock holder's side when a lock is released under contention. This allows for more accurate hold time estimation and provides additional information about the lock holder's stack at release time.

review_comments.author: Andrew Morton
review_comments.summary: Shared an AI review link to help the author address feedback in the next revision.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Wed, 18 Mar 2026 18:45:17 +0000 Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com> wrote:

> The existing contention_begin/contention_end tracepoints fire on the
> waiter side. The lock holder's identity and stack can be captured at
> contention_begin time (e.g. perf lock contention --lock-owner), but
> this reflects the holder's state when a waiter arrives, not when the
> lock is actually released.
> 
> This series adds a contended_release tracepoint that fires on the
> holder side when a lock with waiters is released. This provides:
> 
> - Hold time estimation: when the holder's own acquisition was
>   contended, its contention_end (acquisition) and contended_release
>   can be correlated to measure how long the lock was held under
>   contention.
> 
> - The holder's stack at release time, which may differ from what perf lock
>   contention --lock-owner captures if the holder does significant work between
>   the waiter's arrival and the unlock.
> 
> The series is structured as follows:
> 
> 1. Remove unnecessary linux/sched.h include from trace/events/lock.h.
> 2. Extract __percpu_up_read() out of the inline percpu_up_read() to
>    avoid binary size increase from adding a tracepoint.
> 3. Add contended_release tracepoint and instrument sleepable locks:
>    mutex, rtmutex, semaphore, rwsem, percpu-rwsem, and rwbase_rt.

AI review:
	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/cover.1773858853.git.d@ilvokhin.com

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [PATCH v3 0/4] locking: contended_release tracepoint instrumentation

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v4 0/3] pull region-specific logic into new files

version_history.subject: [PATCH v4 0/3] pull region-specific logic into new files

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 1/3] cxl/core/region: move pmem region driver logic into region_pmem.c
series_items.patch_summary: This patch moves the logic for managing persistent memory (pmem) regions from the overloaded region.c file into a new, dedicated region_pmem.c file. This separation makes it clear that this code only applies to pmem regions and helps to declutter the original file. No functional changes are made as part of this refactoring effort.

review_comments.author: Alison Schofield
review_comments.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch always adds region_pmem.o regardless of CONFIG_CXL_PMEM, and questioned whether the intention is to conditionally compile it based on this configuration option.
review_comments.raw_body:
This always adds region_pmem.o regardless of CONFIG_CXL_PMEM.
Are you heading towards conditionally compiling, where region_pmem.o is
omitted if !CONFIG_CXL_PMEM? 

Similar question for region_dax.o

--Alison

review_comments.author: Gregory Price (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledges reviewer's concern about adding more Kconfig cruft, but doesn't commit to a fix; instead suggests that the current approach might be acceptable despite potential bloat for some deployments.
review_comments.raw_body:
I hadn't considered going that far, but it doesn't seem unreasonable?

It just seemed natural to break out the code this way, but I don't know
if adding more Kconfig cruft is worth it just to save some .o's and a
smaller amount of code.

The recent pmem/nvdimm Kconfig change does kind of show that we might
have some bloat for some deployments which might not care about pmem -
for example.

~Gregory

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 2/3] cxl/core/region: move dax region device logic into region_dax.c
series_items.patch_summary: This patch moves the CXL DAX region device logic from the overloaded region.c file into a new region_dax.c file, allowing for additional dax-driver integration paths without further dirtying the core region.c logic. The move includes the creation of a new region_dax.c file and modifications to the Makefile and core.h files. No functional changes are made by this patch.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 3/3] cxl/core: use cleanup.h for devm_cxl_add_dax_region
series_items.patch_summary: This patch updates the function devm_cxl_add_dax_region to use cleanup.h for device management, replacing goto statements with more modern and efficient error handling using macros from cleanup.h. This change does not introduce any functional changes but improves code quality by reducing the use of gotos.

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch refactors the cxl_region management code in region.c into three new files: region_dax.c, region_pmem.c, and cleanup.h. The goal is to make it clear that certain logic applies only to specific types of regions, reducing development conflicts and improving code clarity.
